title: rubus foliosus subsp. corymbosus
intro: Rubus foliosus subsp. corymbosus, also known as the thimbleberry, is a type of bramble plant that belongs to the rose family (Rosaceae). The thimbleberry is a shrub that produces edible, bright red fruits rich in antioxidants and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties, which can help alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and boost the immune system, offering various health benefits such as improved cardiovascular health and reduced risk of certain cancers. Thimbleberry can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or dried herb, and is often used in herbal remedies due to its versatility and potential health benefits.
